questions.q: Who was Mandodari in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Mandodari was the wise and virtuous chief queen of Ravana, the king of Lanka.

questions.q: In which kingdom did Mandodari live as queen?
questions.explanation: As Ravana's chief queen, Mandodari resided in the golden city of Lanka.

questions.q: Mandodari repeatedly urged Ravana to do what regarding Sita?
questions.explanation: Mandodari pleaded with Ravana in vain to return Sita to Rama and avoid disaster.

questions.q: Which famous warrior of Lanka was Mandodari's son?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, also called Meghanada, was the mighty son of Ravana and Mandodari.

questions.q: What is Mandodari most commonly remembered for in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Mandodari is celebrated as a wise and virtuous queen despite being married to Ravana.

questions.q: What did Mandodari foresee would happen to Lanka if Ravana kept Sita?
questions.explanation: Mandodari warned that keeping Sita would bring about the ruin of Lanka.

questions.q: Mandodari's husband Ravana was the king of which race of beings?
questions.explanation: Ravana, Mandodari's husband, was the powerful ten-headed king of the rakshasas.

questions.q: How is Mandodari generally portrayed in her character within the epic?
questions.explanation: Mandodari is portrayed as a noble, devoted wife who counsels her husband toward righteousness.

questions.q: Whose abduction did Mandodari condemn as a grave wrong by Ravana?
questions.explanation: Mandodari condemned the abduction of Sita, Rama's wife, as a wrong that doomed Lanka.

questions.q: What was Mandodari's relationship to the warrior Indrajit?
questions.explanation: Mandodari was the mother of Indrajit (Meghanada), Ravana's heroic eldest son.

questions.q: According to tradition, whose daughter was Mandodari?
questions.explanation: Mandodari was the daughter of Mayasura, the great architect of the asuras, and the apsara Hema.

questions.q: Mandodari is traditionally counted among which revered group of women?
questions.explanation: Mandodari is honored as one of the Panchakanya, five exemplary women whose names are recited as purifying.

questions.q: When Ravana was killed, what did Mandodari do?
options: Lamented and mourned over his body
Took up arms against the vanaras
questions.explanation: After Ravana fell to Rama, Mandodari delivered a sorrowful lament over her dead husband's body.

questions.q: In her lament, what cause did Mandodari assign for Ravana's downfall?
questions.explanation: Mandodari blamed Ravana's lustful seizure of Sita and his refusal to heed counsel for his ruin.

questions.q: Whose advice did Mandodari often echo when both counseled Ravana to return Sita?
questions.explanation: Like Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother, Mandodari urged the king to return Sita and make peace.

questions.q: What quality of Ravana did Mandodari acknowledge even while criticizing his actions?
questions.explanation: Mandodari praised Ravana's immense strength, scholarship, and devotion to Shiva even as she mourned his folly.

questions.q: Mandodari warned Ravana that Rama was no ordinary man but likely what?
questions.explanation: Mandodari suspected Rama was a divine incarnation, warning that no mortal could perform his feats.

questions.q: What did Mandodari point to as evidence of Rama's might when warning Ravana?
options: His building of a bridge across the ocean
questions.explanation: Mandodari cited the bridge across the sea and the slaughter of Lanka's warriors as proof of Rama's divine power.

questions.q: How did Mandodari respond emotionally to the deaths of her kinsmen during the war?
questions.explanation: Mandodari grieved deeply as the war claimed her son Indrajit and other kin before Ravana's own death.

questions.q: Mandodari is praised in the epic chiefly for her devotion as what?
questions.explanation: Mandodari is extolled as a pativrata, a faithful and chaste wife loyal to Ravana to the end.

questions.q: What did Mandodari urge Ravana to value above his pride when Sita was held captive?
options: Dharma and the safety of Lanka
questions.explanation: Mandodari counseled Ravana to uphold dharma and protect Lanka rather than cling to his pride and lust.

questions.q: Mandodari's father Maya was renowned in tradition as a master of what?
questions.explanation: Mayasura, Mandodari's father, was the celebrated architect and illusionist of the asuras.

questions.q: According to a widespread tradition, whom did Mandodari marry after Ravana's death?
questions.explanation: In a well-known tradition, Vibhishana, who became king of Lanka, married Mandodari after Ravana's death.

questions.q: In some regional retellings of the Ramayana, Mandodari is said to be a hidden mother of which figure?
questions.explanation: Certain regional versions, such as the Adbhuta Ramayana, depict Sita as having been born to or abandoned by Mandodari.

questions.q: What was the name of Mandodari's mother in tradition, an apsara wed to Maya?
questions.explanation: Mandodari's mother was the apsara Hema, wife of the asura architect Maya.

questions.q: Besides Indrajit, which other son is named in tradition as born to Ravana and Mandodari?
questions.explanation: Akshayakumara, slain by Hanuman in Lanka, is traditionally named as a son of Ravana and Mandodari.

questions.q: In Valmiki's text, after Ravana's death Mandodari recalls a curse that doomed him for assaulting which woman?
questions.explanation: Tradition links Ravana's doom to his assault of the chaste Vedavati, who vowed to be reborn as his destroyer (Sita).

questions.q: The name 'Mandodari' is traditionally interpreted to mean a woman with what characteristic?
questions.explanation: The name Mandodari is often glossed as 'one with a soft, slender belly,' reflecting her grace and beauty.

questions.q: When Ravana lay dead, Mandodari reproached him for ignoring the warnings of whom in particular?
questions.explanation: Mandodari reproached Ravana for spurning the wise counsel of his brother Vibhishana, who had urged returning Sita.

questions.q: In the Panchakanya verse, Mandodari is invoked alongside Ahalya, Draupadi, Tara, and which fifth woman?
questions.explanation: The Panchakanya are Ahalya, Draupadi, Kunti (or Sita in some versions), Tara, and Mandodari, recited as sin-destroying names.
